title: pressure cooker mushroom risotto
ingredients: 1.5 lbs assorted mushrooms (a mixture is best), cleaned, trimmed, and thinly sliced
1/2 cup white wine or sake (or just a little water or stock to deglaze)
more parmesan and any finely minced herb for serving
instructions: If using dried mushrooms, heat the stock and allow the dried mushrooms to steep until soft, about 5 minutes for porcini or up to 1 hour for shiitake. Remove hydrated mushrooms and roughly chop. Add mushroom scraps back to the stock and let sit until ready to use.
Heat olive oil and butter in the pressure cooker and add all of the mushrooms. Cook until the moisture is released reduced slightly. It doesn't need to be to dry and browning, but that's fine too.
Add onion and garlic and cook until barely softened. Add rice and cook until starting to become translucent around the edges, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tamari (or soy sauce) and miso paste until evenly distributed. Add wine and cook until the alcohol has cooked off, about 2–3 minutes.
Pour the stock into the pot through a mesh strainer, discarding the mushroom scraps. Seal and cook on low pressure for 5 minutes (about 10 psi). Release the steam valve and open the pressure cooker.
Stir in parmesan and check seasoning. Adjust consistency if needed.
Serve with extra grated parmesan and herbs at the table.
notes: Pairs well with honey butter roast carrots. The dried mushrooms are nice but not necessary and can be skipped. If the risotto turns out too wet, consider using slightly less broth, especially when adding wine or sake. If too dry, loosen with a bit of stock or cream.
